items.title: Kering shares fall 5% as Barclays flags full-year guidance at risk
items.summary: Barclays previewed Kering's July 28 H1 results, projecting Gucci organic revenue at -5% for Q2 (an improvement on Q1's -8%) but forecasting Europe down roughly -10% versus North America's +8%, and cut its confidence that Kering hits full-year targets. Kering shares fell around 5% on the note. Barclays called the 2026 targets increasingly difficult to achieve.

items.title: Action Kering: est-ce le moment d'investir en 2026?
items.summary: French financial analysis piece notes Kering trading near 248 EUR (52-week range 190-354 EUR), average 12-month analyst target 282 EUR implying about 14% upside, but overall rating neutral with a 'strong sell' daily technical signal. Piece frames early-2026 signs of stabilization as still too fragile to confirm a genuine turnaround ahead of the July 28 earnings release.

items.title: Gucci Shifts Into High Gear With F1 Team Sponsorship, New Racing Platform
items.summary: WWD's report on Gucci becoming title partner of the Alpine F1 team from 2027 under a roughly $150 million multi-year deal, the first time a luxury fashion house has held an F1 title sponsorship. Gucci CEO Francesca Bellettini called it a 'very bold, very Gucci move,' with creative director Demna personally involved in team livery and apparel design, launched under a new 'Gucci Racing' platform.

items.title: Is Gucci closing its Avenue Montaigne boutique in Paris?
items.summary: Gucci's roughly 1,500sqm flagship at 60 Avenue Montaigne, occupied about 20 years, may not have its lease renewed amid a rent dispute with the property's owning family, part of a wider Kering retail-footprint reduction (32 points of sale closed globally last year). Gucci is meanwhile preparing a new flagship at Rue Saint-Honore/Rue Castiglione, signaling relocation rather than a full Paris exit.

items.title: Gucci perd un quart de sa valeur au classement Brand Finance Italy 2026
items.summary: Brand Finance's Italy 100 2026 ranking shows Gucci's brand value collapsing 25.3% to 7.6 billion euros, dropping the house from third to fifth among Italian brands, while the overall Italian brand index grew just 1% versus Germany +8%, UK +6% and Spain +11%. Armani (-30.8%) and Prada (-13.7%) also declined, underscoring sector-wide pressure on Italian luxury rather than a Gucci-specific problem alone.

items.title: Gucci official campaign post drawing mixed comment threads
items.summary: Recent official Gucci Instagram campaign post generated comment threads split between admiration for the styling attitude and recurring complaints in replies about price and 'style over substance' since Demna's arrival; brand-owned promo copy itself reads as descriptive rather than evaluative. Engagement counters were not visible on the fetched view.

items.title: Continued Instagram/X commentary on Gucci's AI-generated campaign imagery
items.summary: Outlet compiling ongoing public comment threads on Instagram and X reacting to Gucci's AI-generated 'Primavera' promotional images: recurring criticism that a house charging up to 10,000 dollars for bags has little credibility cutting costs via AI imagery ('Gucci can't find a real human Milanese grandmother'), alongside a smaller share of positive comments calling the visuals striking. Reported as an ongoing trust-in-luxury-advertising debate rather than a single-day spike.

items.title: PurseForum thread: Gucci quality concerns
items.summary: Ongoing PurseForum thread where members report craftsmanship complaints on recent Gucci leather goods - peeling metallic finish, loose hardware screws, uneven stitching - alongside members who defend Gucci's classic loafers and all-leather pieces as still best-in-class. Thread reflects a genuine, unresolved quality-perception split among long-time buyers rather than a one-sided pile-on.

items.title: PurseForum thread: Unpopular Gucci Opinions
items.summary: Community thread airing contrarian views on Gucci under Demna: some posters say the brand has 'lost its identity' chasing hype cycles and Tom-Ford nostalgia rather than innovating, others push back that the reset was necessary after the Michele-to-De Sarno whiplash. Mix of nostalgia-driven negativity and cautious openness to the new direction.

items.title: LinkedIn commentary on Gucci's Brand Finance value drop
items.summary: French luxury-sector LinkedIn post reacting to Gucci's 25% Brand Finance Italy value decline, arguing it reflects a structural loss of desirability built up over the De Sarno years rather than a one-off, and questioning whether Demna's reset can reverse the trend quickly enough for Kering's 2026 targets. Drew professional-audience discussion in the comments, mostly analytical rather than emotional.
